creator: rotkap@gmx.de
text:
Matthias Andree <matthias.andree@gmx.de> wrote:

> I have also tried to reproduce your clementine problems, to no avail.
>
> I tried to build and install and package clementine-player on 9.2-amd64
> (not converted to pkgNG), which worked, and I used poudriere to
> test-build clementine-player on 9.1-amd64, which was also successful.

Maybe a bug in pkg New generation?

> I also checked the 3rdparty/libmplayer sources, and the CMakeLists.txt
> pretty clearly states an installation directory that matches the pkg-plist.
>
> I do not see how adding NO_STAGE would make a difference - if you can
> upload compressed logs of a build made with NO_STAGE, and of a build
> without NO_STAGE, and a list of your options ("make
> pretty-print-config"), that might help in finding the problem.

Okay 

[root@ahab /usr/ports/audio/clementine-player]# make pretty-print-config
-AUDIOCD -BOX -BREAKPAD -DEVICEKIT -DROPBOX -GIO -GOOGLE_DRIVE -GPOD -LASTFM +MOODBAR -MTP -VISUALISATION -WIIMOTEDEV 

> Else I am tempted to believe that _some_ part in your build is
> stale.

Terrible to find that.

Maybe pkg new generation? 

> If NFS or thereabouts are involved, clocks may have drifted, if you use
> compilation accelerators/caches like ccache, that might also impair the
> build.

No and no.


Please see here again:

http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=185597


the installer ist looking in
/usr/ports/audio/clementine-player/work/stage/usr/local/share/clementine/projectm-presets/


but the .milk file arent there, they are in 
/work/clementine-1.2.1/3rdparty/libprojectm/presets/


Why the installer is looking in the "wrong" directory?

attachhed: the make logs



with_stage.tar.gz
no_stage.tar.gz

Heino
